leagueBriefing: Real Madrid and Atlético Madrid both tightened their grip on the top three with crucial home victories, while the middle of the table saw a massive shake-up. Los Blancos dismantled Elche to close within a single point of leaders Barcelona, headlined by a sensational Arda Güler goal from behind the halfway line. Meanwhile, Girona produced a dominant three-goal showing to humble Athletic Club, and Real Oviedo climbed out of the basement with a gritty win over a struggling Valencia side.

games.headline: Girona clinical in dominant three-goal rout of Athletic Club
games.brief: Girona delivered one of their most complete performances of the season at Montilivi, systematically dismantling a top-six hopeful in Athletic Club. The hosts struck early through Hugo Rincón and never looked back, adding two late insurance goals to turn a competitive match into a blowout and move up to 15th in the table.
bullets: Hugo Rincón opened the scoring just four minutes in with his first goal of the season.
Azzedine Ounahi doubled the lead in the 77th minute following a clinical fast break.
Claudio Echeverri sealed the win in stoppage time with a clever finish from close range.
Girona goalkeeper Paulo Gazzaniga made eight saves to keep an unlikely clean sheet against 20 Athletic shots.
games.context: The victory pulls Girona five points clear of the relegation zone, while Athletic Club misses a chance to jump into the European spots.

games.headline: Molina stunner secures derby spoils for Atlético Madrid
games.brief: In a typically cagey Madrid derby, it took a moment of pure individual brilliance from Nahuel Molina to separate the sides. Atlético Madrid struggled to break down Getafe's disciplined five-man backline for much of the afternoon, but Molina's goal-of-the-season contender was enough to reclaim third place for Diego Simeone's men.
bullets: Nahuel Molina scored the only goal with a spectacular long-range strike that nestled in the top corner.
Jan Oblak recorded his 15th clean sheet of the season, leading all La Liga goalkeepers.
Atlético Madrid has now won 25 of their last 34 meetings against their local rivals Getafe.
Getafe's Jose Bordalás remains winless in 17 career league matches against Atlético.
games.context: Atleti moves back into third place, maintaining their unbeaten home record and staying within five points of the title race.

games.headline: Costas header lifts Oviedo off bottom in tense win over Valencia
games.brief: Real Oviedo breathed new life into their survival hopes with a gutsy 1-0 win over Valencia at the Carlos Tartiere. David Costas provided the decisive header in the first half, and a heroic performance from goalkeeper Aarón Escandell ensured the hosts held on despite a late VAR scare that overturned what would have been a second Oviedo goal.
bullets: David Costas scored the winning goal in the 30th minute with a powerful header from a set piece.
Aarón Escandell was named Man of the Match after making six crucial saves to preserve the win.
Alberto Reina had a goal ruled out by VAR for offside in the 77th minute.
The win marks Real Oviedo's first home victory in their last five league attempts.
games.context: Oviedo moves off the bottom of the table for the first time in weeks, while Valencia remains mired in mid-table mediocrity.

games.headline: Güler scores from halfway line as Madrid crush Elche
games.brief: Real Madrid moved within a point of the top of the table after a dominant 4-1 victory over Elche at the Santiago Bernabéu. While Antonio Rüdiger and Fede Valverde provided the foundation, the night belonged to Arda Güler, who capped a heavy rotation performance with an audacious 50-yard strike that will be talked about for years to come.
bullets: Arda Güler scored a wonder-goal in the 89th minute, lobbing the keeper from behind the halfway line.
Antonio Rüdiger opened the scoring in the 38th minute following a scramble in the box.
Real Madrid were permitted an unusual sixth substitution due to concussion protocols after a clash of heads.
Fede Valverde continued his scoring streak with a trademark long-range rocket just before halftime.
games.context: Madrid is now just one point behind Barcelona in the title race, setting up a massive sprint finish for the domestic crown.
